specific_mechanism: Frequency seriation orders archaeological assemblages by stylistic similarity: each style follows a bell-curve popularity over time; assemblages with similar style frequencies are temporally close. The Brainerd-Robinson coefficient rearranges similarity matrices so that the diagonal contains the strongest similarities and similarities decrease monotonically away from it; correspondence analysis is the modern variant
llm_application: An ordering mechanism for multi-agent message streams: each agent's message style follows a popularity bell-curve over its lifetime; the inter-agent communication log is rearranged via Brainerd-Robinson similarity to recover the implicit temporal ordering of agent activity, even when message timestamps are unreliable or absent. The mechanism gives a structural recovery of communication chronology from style alone.
novelty_claim: Existing multi-agent log analysis uses explicit timestamps or topic models. No published 2024-2026 LLM/AI work imports Brainerd-Robinson frequency-seriation-style chronological recovery from message style frequencies alone.
